methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $7.0B - $300.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: OpenAI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $300B+

private.name: Anthropic
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $183B

private.name: Anysphere
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $29.3B

private.name: Neuralink
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $9.7B

private.name: Groq
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $7B

data.narrative:
Alpha School is a self-funded AI & Software company founded by MacKenzie Price and Brian Holtz at the private stage, with a $1B Liemandt commitment. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of five private-stage AI & Software companies: OpenAI ($300.0B), Anthropic ($183.0B), Anysphere ($29.3B), Neuralink ($9.7B), and Groq ($7.0B).

Peer-median valuation methodology was applied given Alpha School's alignment with this cohort on sector and stage. The median valuation across these five comparables is $29.3B, with a measured range spanning $7.0B (Groq floor) to $300.0B (OpenAI ceiling). This method yields an implied valuation of $29.3B for the target.

The triangulated valuation range is $24.9B to $33.7B, centered on the $29.3B median. This analysis rests on a single applied methodology; incorporation of revenue multiples, discounted cash flow, or transaction precedent would strengthen conviction but such data are not available. Key uncertainties include Alpha School's revenue, growth trajectory, and competitive positioning within the frontier AI market—all material to valuation but absent from this dataset. The wide dispersion among peers ($7.0B–$300.0B) underscores the heterogeneity of private AI company valuations and suggests material downside or upside risk depending on where Alpha School's capabilities and business model ultimately position it within the sector.
